title: Programming the Hull Pixelbot
description: Write sequential MicroPython programs to make the Hull Pixelbot move, light up, and react to its surroundings — no framework required.

labs.title: Hardware Required
labs.desc: Find out what hardware you need to create a robot

labs.title: Lab 1: Pixel Colours
labs.desc: Set up the robot library and light the pixel strip with named colours

labs.title: Lab 2: Colour Sequence
labs.desc: Use a list and a loop to cycle through colours with time.sleep pacing

labs.title: Lab 3: Pixel Patterns
labs.desc: Control individual pixels to build patterns and chasing effects

labs.title: Lab 1: Basic Movement
labs.desc: Move the robot forward, backward, and turn it on the spot

labs.title: Lab 2: Nowait and Wait
labs.desc: Start a move in the background and use wait() to synchronise later

labs.title: Lab 3: Speed Control
labs.desc: Use the seconds= parameter to control how long a move takes

labs.title: Lab 4: Arc Movement
labs.desc: Move the robot along curved paths using arc()

labs.title: Lab 5: Movement Sequences
labs.desc: Combine moves, turns, and arcs to drive programmed shapes

labs.title: Lab 1: Reading Distance
labs.desc: Wire the HC-SR04, take readings, and print distances to the console

labs.title: Lab 2: Conditional Colour
labs.desc: Change the pixel colour based on how far away an object is

labs.title: Lab 3: Obstacle Avoidance
labs.desc: Make the robot back away and turn whenever it gets too close to something

labs.title: Lab 4: Explore
labs.desc: Combine everything you have learned to give the robot a behaviour of your own design

labs.title: Lab 1: The Sumo Game
labs.desc: Learn the arena layout, rules, scoring, and the three-phase program skeleton

labs.title: Lab 2: Charging
labs.desc: Drive straight at full speed for the entire game — the simplest possible tactic

labs.title: Lab 3: Dodging
labs.desc: Use the distance sensor to detect the opponent and swerve around them

labs.title: Lab 4: Curving
labs.desc: Approach from the side using alternating arcs, switching to a charge when close

labs.title: Robot Library Reference
labs.desc: A complete reference for all robot library functions — movement, pixels, distance sensor, and utilities
